title: Bring self-host checker semantics closer to Go checker parity
goal: Expand selfhost/checker.tya so generated self-host tools reject the same important invalid programs as the Go checker for the supported subset.
context: Start with parser node support already available after SELFHOST-003.
Use internal/checker tests as the source of truth for semantics where practical.
Focus on scoped names, invalid bindings, duplicate definitions, return/loop constraints, and module-related constraints only when supported by parser nodes.
steps: Pick the next checker parity gap from SELFHOST_WORK.md.
Add fixture coverage that demonstrates both accepted and rejected programs.
Implement the checker behavior in selfhost/checker.tya.
Ensure generated stage tools preserve the same checker result for the covered fixture.
done_when: One checker parity gap is closed with tests.
Generated self-host checker behavior matches the expected supported-subset semantics.
